description:
Implement the per-worker review pass for current-branch workers: a reviewer agent that inspects ticket-attributed commits using its own tools.

## What to implement

The current-branch reviewer should:
1. Receive a review prompt with: ticket description, epic/scope goal, attributed commit SHAs with one-line messages, worker handoff comments, coordination context
2. Use its own tools (read, grep, `git show`, etc.) to inspect commits in detail
3. Produce review findings concrete enough for coordinator triage

## Reusing Existing Infrastructure

The existing codebase has reviewer infrastructure in `orchestrator.ts`:
- `runReviewerPass()` — launches a reviewer agent with specific command/config
- `buildReviewerCommand()` / `resolveReviewerSettings()` — command/model/provider resolution
- `parseReviewOutput()` — extracts structured review findings from raw agent output

Reuse this infrastructure where possible. Key adaptations:
- The review PROMPT changes significantly (commit-list-centric, not a diff bundle)
- The reviewer COMMAND resolution can be reused as-is (same agent binary, same model config)
- The review OUTPUT parsing may need adjustment if the structured format differs
- Existing reviewer timeouts and error handling apply

## Key Differences from Worktree Review

- Reviewer does NOT receive a pre-computed diff bundle or truncated patch artifact
- Reviewer does NOT review `launchHead..HEAD` wholesale
- `maxArtifactChars` does NOT apply to the primary review input (may still apply to log excerpts)
- The review prompt is small and commit-list-centric
- The reviewer is a full pi agent with tools, inspecting the actual codebase
- Review evaluates work quality and correctness, not "is this safe to merge"

## Design Rules

- Per-worker review is ON by default for current-branch mode (controlled by `config.workerExecution.review.enabled`)
- Skip ONLY when `config.workerExecution.review.enabled === false`
- `landing.review.enabled = false` must NOT cause current-branch review to be skipped
- Reviewer prompt describes this as a review of ticket-attributed commits on the current branch, NOT as a merge-back gate
- Reviewer findings must be concrete: what file, what issue, what should change
- Reuse existing reviewer command/agent infrastructure (provider/model config, timeouts)

## Tests

- Per-worker review runs for every completed current-branch worker by default
- Review is skipped only when `config.workerExecution.review.enabled === false`
- `landing.review.enabled = false` does NOT skip current-branch review
- Review prompt contains ticket context and commit SHAs
- Review prompt does NOT contain a truncated diff bundle
- Review prompt does NOT reference `launchHead..HEAD` wholesale
- Reviewer output is parseable into triage-ready findings
- Review timeout is respected

## Definition of Done

- Current-branch review pass implemented and callable from `verifyCurrentBranchWorker`
- Reviewer receives commit-list prompt, uses tools to inspect
- ON by default, skippable only with explicit config
- Reuses existing reviewer command/model resolution
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean

comments.text: Implementation note: The reviewer agent output must be parseable into structured findings for coordinator triage (task .3.4). Define a `ReviewFinding` type that captures: `{ file: string, issue: string, suggestion: string, severity: "fix" | "nit" }`. The coordinator (not the reviewer) classifies findings as fix/file/reject. The reviewer just reports concrete issues. The existing `parseReviewOutput()` function parses `<review_report>` blocks — adapt this pattern for current-branch review output, or evolve the output format if needed.

title: Implement per-worker review for current-branch workers (agent-driven, not diff-bundle)
